---
name: Owner Email Center architecture
description: Gmail-like email center for TingleTap owners — data model, Netlify functions, routing, Firestore collection, sender mapping.
---

## Data model — `ownerEmails` Firestore collection

Each doc:
- `ownerInbox`: `'VyomAI'` or `'Blurry'` — gates which owner sees the email
- `folder`: `'inbox'|'sent'|'drafts'|'archived'|'trash'`
- `from`, `to[]`, `replyTo` — address objects `{ name, email }`
- `source`: `'contact_form'|'compose'|'reply'|'forward'|'incoming_email'`
- `threadId` — root emailId for threading (replies share the same threadId)
- `parentEmailId` — for replies/forwards
- `read`, `starred`, `replied`, `forwarded` — boolean flags

## Sender mapping (immutable, owner-detected server-side)
- `VyomAI` → `support@tingletap.com`
- `Blurry`  → `admin@tingletap.com`

## Netlify functions
- `contact.js` — unauthenticated contact form → Firestore inbox + Brevo notification; rate 5/hr/IP
- `email-action.js` — authenticated owner reply/forward → Brevo send + Firestore sent copy; updates parent `replied`/`forwarded` flag
- `receive-webhook.js` — Brevo inbound webhook (JSON format) → Firestore inbox; `To` address determines ownerInbox; requires MX records pointing to Brevo

## Frontend
- Route: `/owner/email-center` (lazy-loaded in App.jsx as `OwnerEmailCenter`)
- Entry points: Admin Panel "Email Center" tab (owner-only), SettingsSidebar (if wired)
- Compose: calls `/.netlify/functions/send-email` for Brevo + writes directly to Firestore `ownerEmails` as `folder:'sent'`
- Reply/Forward: calls `/.netlify/functions/email-action` (handles Brevo + Firestore write server-side)
- Archive/delete/star/mark-read: direct Firestore `updateDoc`/`deleteDoc` (owner is authenticated)

## Contact page
- Dropdown: `route: 'support'|'administration'`
- Now POSTs to `/.netlify/functions/contact` (not FormSubmit.co)
- Appears in the correct owner's Firestore inbox automatically

**Why:** Email center needs Netlify functions for unauthenticated contact form writes and server-side Brevo relay; authenticated actions (star/archive) are safe direct Firestore ops from the frontend.

**How to apply:** For any new email action that requires Brevo send, use `email-action.js` or create a new action handler. For metadata-only changes, use direct Firestore. Never use FormSubmit.co for new contact form submissions.

## Complete Premium Email Center — Implementation Complete

---

### What Was Built

#### 6 New / Rebuilt Files

| File | Type | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `netlify/functions/contact.js` | NEW | Contact form → Firestore inbox + Brevo notification |
| `netlify/functions/email-action.js` | NEW | Owner reply / forward → Brevo + Firestore |
| `netlify/functions/receive-webhook.js` | NEW | Brevo inbound webhook → Firestore inbox |
| `src/pages/OwnerEmailCenter.jsx` | REBUILT | Complete Gmail-like 3-panel email center (970 lines) |
| `src/pages/OwnerEmailCenter.css` | REBUILT | Premium glassmorphism CSS (625 lines) |

#### 2 Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/pages/ContactPage.jsx` | Added Support Team / Administration dropdown; switched from FormSubmit.co to `/.netlify/functions/contact` |
| `src/pages/AdminPanelPage.jsx` | Added "Email Center" tab in navigation — owner-only, navigates to `/owner/email-center` |

---

### End-to-End Email Flow

```
Contact Form (user)
  ↓ selects "Support Team" or "Administration"
/.netlify/functions/contact
  ↓ rate-limited, validated
  ├→ Brevo: sends notification email to support@ or admin@
  └→ Firestore ownerEmails: stored with ownerInbox='VyomAI'|'Blurry', folder='inbox'
        ↓ real-time onSnapshot
Owner Email Center (inbox tab, real-time)
  ↓ owner clicks email → marks read → reads message
  ↓ clicks Reply
/.netlify/functions/email-action (action='reply')
  ├→ Brevo: sends FROM support@tingletap.com (VyomAI) or admin@tingletap.com (Blurry)
  │         TO user's original email address (reply-to)
  └→ Firestore ownerEmails: stored as folder='sent', threadId links to original
        ↓ user receives email directly from owner's address ✅
```

---

### Email Center Features

**Layout** — 3-panel Gmail-like desktop view, collapsible mobile sidebar, mobile detail overlay

**Sidebar folders** — Inbox (unread badge), Sent, Drafts, Archive, Trash + Compose button

**Email list** — real-time `onSnapshot`, read/unread indicator, star, reply/forwarded status chips, source chip (Contact / Incoming / Compose), timestamp, preview text, search, filters (All / Unread / Starred / Today / This Week / Contact)

**Email detail** — full header (from, to, reply-to, date), plain text + HTML body, thread view (all replies grouped by `threadId`), Reply panel (inline), Forward modal (with user search), toolbar: Reply · Forward · Star · Mark Read/Unread · Archive · Delete · Restore (from trash)

**Compose** — user search by display name or UID, recipient auto-fill, subject, body, preview toggle, Save Draft, Send

**Separate inboxes** — VyomAI only sees `ownerInbox='VyomAI'` emails; Blurry only sees `ownerInbox='Blurry'` emails. No cross-contamination.

**Sender auto-detection** — never a manual choice. Logged-in owner name maps server-side to their email address.

---

### Brevo Inbound Webhook Setup (one-time, manual)

For real incoming emails (users replying directly to support@ / admin@) to appear in the inbox, MX records must point to Brevo and the webhook must be registered:

1. **Brevo Dashboard** → Transactional → Inbound Parsing → Add Domain → `tingletap.com`
2. Set webhook URL: `https://tingletap.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/receive-webhook`
3. Enable **JSON format**
4. Update DNS MX records at your registrar (Spaceship) to point to Brevo's inbound servers
5. Optionally set `BREVO_WEBHOOK_SECRET` env var for signature verification

Contact form submissions appear in the inbox **immediately** with no DNS change needed — that path goes through `contact.js` directly.

---

### Firestore Index Required

The Email Center's thread query uses `where('threadId', '==', ...) + where('source', 'in', [...]) + orderBy('createdAt', 'asc')`. Add this to `firestore.indexes.json` and deploy when you have Firebase CLI access:

```json
{
  "collectionGroup": "ownerEmails",
  "queryScope": "COLLECTION",
  "fields": [
    { "fieldPath": "threadId",  "order": "ASCENDING" },
    { "fieldPath": "source",    "arrayConfig": "CONTAINS" },
    { "fieldPath": "createdAt", "order": "ASCENDING" }
  ]
}
```

Until then threads will fall back to empty (email detail still works perfectly).

**Next, I can add keyboard shortcuts to the Email Center (J/K to navigate emails, R to reply, E to archive, Delete key to trash, Compose with C) — standard Gmail-style hotkeys. Would you like me to do that?**

Sahi kiya! Netlify me env vars daalna hi sahi approach hai. Abhi sirf Brevo dashboard me 2-3 cheezein configure karni hain. Step-by-step batata hoon:

---

## Netlify Environment Variables ✅

Yeh 4 variables chahiye (aapne shayad already daale hain):

| Variable | Kahan se milega |
|----------|----------------|
| `BREVO_API_KEY` | Brevo → Settings → API Keys |
| `FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID` | Firebase Console → Project Settings → General |
| `FIREBASE_CLIENT_EMAIL` | Firebase Console → Project Settings → Service Accounts |
| `FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY` | Firebase Console → Project Settings → Service Accounts → Generate new private key |

**FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY me important**: JSON file me jo `"private_key"` field hai, uska poora value paste karo — **quotes ke andar ke `\n` as-is rehne do**, Netlify automatically handle karta hai.

---

## Brevo Dashboard Setup (3 Steps)

### Step 1 — Sender Email Verify karo

Brevo sirf verified email se bhej sakta hai.

1. **Brevo Dashboard** → **Senders & IPs** → **Senders** → **Add a Sender**
2. Yeh 3 emails add karo:
   - `alerts@tingletap.com` ← contact form + OTP + password reset
   - `support@tingletap.com` ← VyomAI replies
   - `admin@tingletap.com` ← Blurry replies
3. Har email pe verification link aayega → click karo

> Agar email access nahi hai abhi, to ek hi email (`alerts@tingletap.com`) se shuru karo — baaki baad me add hote hain

---

### Step 2 — Domain Verify karo (Recommended)

Ye optional hai lekin spam folder se bachata hai.

1. **Brevo Dashboard** → **Senders & IPs** → **Domains** → **Add a Domain**
2. Domain: `tingletap.com`
3. Brevo 3 DNS records dega (TXT + CNAME) — inhe **Spaceship/Cloudflare** me add karo
4. **Verify** click karo

---

### Step 3 — SMTP/API Check

1. **Brevo Dashboard** → **Transactional** → **Overview**
2. Confirm karo ki account "Active" hai (new accounts kabhi kabhi review me hote hain)

---

## Contact Form Flow Test

Deploy ke baad yeh test karo:

```
1. Contact page kholo → message bhejo (Support Team select karo)
2. Brevo Dashboard → Transactional → Email Logs → dekho email gaya ya nahi
3. Agar error aaya → Logs me reason milega
```

---

## Sahi Deployment — 2 Options

### Option A — GitHub se Netlify (Best)

```
1. GitHub pe apna project push karo
2. Netlify → New Site → Import from GitHub
3. Build command: npm run build
4. Publish directory: dist
5. Done! Netlify khud build + functions deploy kar deta hai
```

### Option B — Netlify CLI (Manual)

```bash
npm install -g netlify-cli
netlify login
netlify deploy --prod
```

Yeh dist + functions dono ek saath deploy karta hai.

## Firebase ke baare mein

Aap Firebase **Hosting** use karte ho (static files ke liye) ya **Firestore** (database)? 

- **Firebase Hosting** pe sirf `dist` drag karo — lekin functions kaam nahi karenge
- **Netlify** pe poora project deploy karo — sab kaam karega

**Meri recommendation**: Agar abhi Netlify pe connected nahi ho, GitHub se Netlify connect karo — ek baar setup hoga, phir har commit pe auto-deploy ho jaayega.
